History of Present Illness:
Mr. ___ is a ___ y/o M hx of right metacarpal fracture, 
suicide attempts, heroin abuse, who presents with 5 days of mild 
cough that worsened over the past two days. Patient has been 
having fever, nasal congestion, sore throat, along with one 
episode of vomiting and diarrhea. He also reports myalgias, but 
denies any neck stiffness or headache. He came to ___ due to 
these symptoms.  
 In the ED, initial vital signs were:  
 101.9 107 ,148/99 18 97% RA

Hospital Course:
BRIEF SUMMARY
=============
Mr. ___ is a ___ year old male with a hx of suicide 
attempts, metacarpal fracture, opioid abuse, but no other 
significant PMH who presented with malaise and fever and found 
to have influenza A. He was treated with oseltamavir, however 
developed some mild agitation the day of discharge and admitted 
that he was withdrawaling from heroin, his last use being two 
days prior to discharge. He was anxious to leave, and refused to 
stay for further treatment. He was offered intranasal naloxone, 
which he said he had at home and was discharged to follow up 
with his PCP at ___.

ACUTE ISSUES
============
#Influenza: Patient p/w n/v/d, myalgia and cough x5 days. 
Influenza A PCR +. Started on oseltamavir ___. Was given IVF 
and started on duonebs in ED with improvement of symptoms. Was 
hemodynamically stable during his course without O2 requirement. 
He was discharged with remainder of course of oseltamavir. 

CHRONIC ISSUES
==============
#Hx of heroin abuse: On the day of discharge, the patient 
admitted was somewhat agitated and admitted that he was 
withdrawaling from heroin, his last use being two days prior to 
discharge. He was given a dose of clonidine to help with 
symptoms. He stated that he was willing to get back on methadone 
but would discuss this with his primary care doctor. Patient was 
offered intranasal naloxone but stated he already had some at 
home.

#Right Metacarpal Fracture:  
-Stable. Patient followed by orthopedics.
